mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession probability, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of labor market tightness.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and declining interest rates amplifies consumer purchasing power and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS changes yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample tests, or positive returns fail to materialize in >55% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and strong order backlogs, which drives short-term momentum and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during supply-constrained regimes, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, which systematically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of high-GDP-growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation indexed by CPIAUCSL exceeds 195.0, it forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity valuation risk and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 195.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates synchronized economic expansion across services and construction sectors, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline returns, with positive 60-day returns occurring in <50% of instances, or the interaction term shows negative predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing demand and corporate earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns during early recession phases, with positive returns occurring in >60% of instances, or the signal Rank IC turns positive.
